metadata.title:
STOP IT

CITY OFFICIALS TAKE QUICK
ACTION TO PREVENT RAILWAY
COMPANY LAYING TRACK ON
THIRD AvENUE-MATER rs
ORDERED KEMovED-CovER
READY TO ACT.

pages.raw_text:
The board of public works this
morning addresses a letter to the
Washington Water Power company
ordering it to desist from hauling
ties, rails or other building material
TO Third avenue between Sherman
and Napa streets, and to remove
such building material as has been
hauled there. This order is the out
come of the attempt yesterday on
the part of the Washington Wafer
Power company to begin construction
of the Third avenue carline on the
strength Of a franchise granted in
1890, but which Corporation Counsel
Judson holds has expired under the
provision of the chapter of 1891. The
charter under the new ruling pro-
vides that any streetcar franchise
shall lapse unless work is commenced
within so days and complied within
one year after passage.


Take Intot Court.


Fearing that work would be rush
ed through in the night, Third avenue
was patrolled last night under orders





today that the reason of the attempt
ed work on the part of the Washing
ton Water Power company yesterday
was to drag the disputed point into
the courts in order to tie the hands
of the city. 1t is in the power of
the city to issue a restraining order
against the company but Corporation
Counsel Judson considered the order
issued sufficient.


1t is said the city officials took this
action this morning fearing the
Washington Water Power company
would secure a restraining order
against the city to prevent II from
ordering the company TO desist from
hauling material to the proposed
building site. 1t was also stated
that the company might attempt to
rush work on the proposed line in
the absence of city officials from the
city hall tomorrow, or some time
during the night.
